Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Asymptomatic | 2, Pre-symptomatic illness | 3, Mild/Moderate outpatient illness | 4, Acute illness | 5, Severe/Critical inpatient illness | 6, Exposed | 9, Convalescent illiness
Field Annotation:
| From RADx-UP Testing Core.  This replaces covid_test_target_disease_status with a multi select option based on site feedback. Definitions:  Asymptomatic = Being asymptomatic means that you have no symptoms. Pre-symptomatic illness = Time between infection and developing symptoms. This category can only be distinguished from "asymptomatic" if the patient is followed to determine if symptoms ever developed.  Mild/Moderate outpatient illness = Covid positive outpatient.  Acute illness (symptomatic) = Symptomatic COVID disease without regard for severity.  Severe/Critical inpatient illness = Covid positive inpatient.
Exposed = Was in contact with COVID positive person but is themselves asymptomatic at the time of testing.  Convalescent illness = Previously had COVID.
